Contributions.Title: The Top Opportunities of Copilot with Microsoft Dynamics 365
Contributions.Description:
What's Inside

Copilot transforms the way people work and provides unique solutions for many organizations through embedded capabilities throughout the Dynamics 365 and Power Platform suite of applications. Further, we are witnessing the impact of Copilot as it continues to evolve and becomes a natural part of the apps we use each day.

So, this begs the question: What are the opportunities for organizations, teams, and individuals that Copilot creates?

This question, and many others, will be answered throughout this guidebook as we dig into:

How AI is reshaping our flow of work
How Copilot & Dynamics 365 create customer experience opportunities
Top opportunities for salespeople with Copilot in Dynamics 365
Opportunities with Copilot in Dynamics 365 Field Service
How Copilot delivers valuable opportunities with Dynamics 365 Finance & Supply Chain

The future outlook on Agentic AI and the next-generation technologies organizations must navigate in their digital defenses

Contributions.Title: Agentic AI: A Paradigm Shift in Cybersecurity Operations (SOC)
Contributions.Description: The cybersecurity world is on the cusp of a fundamental transformation. “Agentic AI” — the practice of deploying autonomous AI agents throughout our technology stack — promises to revolutionize how security teams operate. This isn’t just another incremental upgrade or new tool; it’s a structural paradigm shift in how we defend, detect, and decide. Industry analysts have dubbed this trend agentic AI, envisioning a virtual workforce of AI-driven agents that augment human teams by taking on day-to-day tasks and decisions.

Contributions.Title: Silverfort: Stopping Scattered Spider with an Identity-First IR Approach
Contributions.Description: Scattered Spider is redefining cyber threats with MFA fatigue, SIM swapping, and social engineering to bypass traditional defenses. By the time alerts surface, they’ve already escalated privileges and moved laterally—leaving organizations scrambling. Learn how an Identity-First IR approach can detect compromised accounts early, enforce adaptive access controls, and automate containment to reduce dwell time and blast radius. Join us for a live response walkthrough and gain actionable insights to outmaneuver modern attackers with an identity-first defense.

Contributions.Title: How to Use TypeSpec for Documenting and Modeling APIs
Contributions.Description:
In my article How to Use TypeSpec for Documenting and Modeling APIs, I present how to use TypeSpec, a declarative language inspired by TypeScript, designed to model and document different types of APIs, such as REST, gRPC or GraphQL. I explain how TypeSpec automatically generates OpenAPI specifications, client code and sample requests, simplifying the creation, documentation and maintenance of robust APIs.

Since its publication, my article has generated a great deal of interest within the developer community.
It has been relayed on several platforms (Twitter, LinkedIn, Facebook, ect.), showing just how much the topic resonates with today's API documentation needs. By highlighting this little-known tool, I'm contributing to the evolution of API modeling and documentation practices, offering a new, more modern, efficient and reliable approach to service development.

Contributions.Title: Blazor WebAssembly versus popular front-end frameworks: which to choose?
Contributions.Description:
In this article, the author compares Blazor WebAssembly with other popular frontend frameworks like React, Angular, and Vue. It explores use cases where Blazor may offer distinct advantages, particularly for C# developers or those integrating .NET applications. The article examines performance, browser compatibility, and specific challenges of Blazor WebAssembly, such as download size and load times. It also provides an analysis of the pros and cons of each approach, helping developers make an informed decision based on their needs.

Impact of this contribution:

Supporting development teams: This article helps developers choose the best technology suited to their needs, whether for familiarity with C# (Blazor) or more established options like React.

Promoting good development practices: By clearly outlining the strengths and limitations of each framework, the article helps teams make more informed choices.

Contributions.Title: Cypress vs Playwright Comparison : Which Tool to Choose for User Interface Testing?
Contributions.Description:
In this article, I compare two of the most popular UI testing tools: Cypress and Playwright. I examine the use cases in which one may be more suitable than the other, taking into account the specific needs of developers and test teams. Accompanied by test results, enabling developers to see the differences in terms of performance and user experience.

Impact of this contribution:

- Supporting development teams: This article enables developers to choose the test tool that best meets their specific needs, whether for ease of use (Cypress) or cross-browser test support (Playwright).

- Promote good testing practices: By offering clear analyses of the performance and usability of each tool, I help teams adopt effective tools that will improve test quality and productivity.

Contributions.Title: Query your Postgres Database Using Azure Functions
Contributions.Description:
In this article, I explain how to use Azure Functions to interact with a PostgreSQL database hosted on Neon using Azure Functions, JavaScript, NodeJS and Visual Studio Code. The aim is to show developers how they can execute SQL queries efficiently. I detail every step, from configuring Neon Database to writing Azure Functions code, managing connections and queries.

Impact of this contribution:

- Encouraging the use of Azure Functions: Many developers don't necessarily know how to exploit this technology for PostgreSQL databases. This guide gives them a solid starting point.

- Promoting best practices: I explain how to manage database connections, avoid the pitfalls of serverless environments and optimize SQL query performance.

- Reach a large community: The article was published on Neon.tech, giving it high visibility among cloud developers and architects looking for high-performance, scalable solutions.

Contributions.Title: NDC Porto: Less is more—a build pipeline for the modern age
Contributions.Description:
In today's world of rich CI and infrastructure tooling, it's often easy to forget about elements of the UNIX philosophy: “Write programs that do one thing and do it well. Write programs to work together.

As it turns out, we can apply this philosophy to our build and CI/CD pipelines just as much as ever. In my personal journey, I've followed the bell curve from from basic building blocks, to rich, integrated systems, and all the way back down again.

Join me and learn how to pick the best building blocks for your build pipeline, with examples in C# and .NET, and above all—how to keep it simple.

Contributions.Title: Techorama NL: Where we're going... we don't need batch jobs
Contributions.Description: Ever write a batch job? Batch jobs are bits of code that periodically look at your database’s current or historic state and then act on it. They often run off-hours or in the middle of the night to avoid performance impact, and if they fail while processing, get ready for a pager alert. You can prevent this nightmare by telling your system what to do in the future! Look to the real world to model a long-running business process from start to finish. Join me and learn: - The challenges with recurring batch jobs. - How to move a business process away from batch processing. - Real-world use cases for modelling future events.

Contributions.Title: .NET Day Switzerland: Five common mistakes with distributed systems
Contributions.Description: Does your system involve more than one computer? Then you have a distributed system. While distributed systems can be highly effective, it's easy to get things wrong. Sometimes, so wrong, it would be better to run everything on one big, expensive, machine. Usually this stems from one or more common mistakes, all of which I've made myself over the years, but as they say—learning comes from doing. Join me and learn how to detect, avoid, and fix these mistakes and streamline your distributed systems for years to come.

Contributions.Title: DevSum: Finding your service boundaries—a practical guide
Contributions.Description:
We know it's useful to split up complex systems. We've seen the benefits of modular deployment of microservices. Dealing with only one piece of code at a time eases our cognitive load. But how do we know where to draw the service boundaries? In complex business domains, it's often difficult to know where to start. When we get our boundaries wrong, the clocks starts ticking. Before long, we hear ourselves say "it would be easier to re-write it".

Join Adam for practical advice on discovering the hidden boundaries in your systems. Help tease out the natural separation of concerns in a sample business domain. During 20 years of developing complex systems, Adam has had plenty of time to get things wrong. Learn to avoid the common pitfalls that can lead us down the path to "the big re-write".

Contributions.Title: Goodbye GIL: Exploring the Free-threaded mode in Python 3.13 and Beyond
Contributions.Description:
The talk explored the free-threaded mode which was introduced in Python 3.13. 

A free-threaded interpreter was set-up using the latest Python 3.14 release candidate and benchmarking was done against the GIL-enabled version for various tasks. The session assessed the impact on single-threaded vs multithreaded code and test the performance across CPU-bound and I/O-bound tasks, aiming to identify scenarios where free-threaded Python excels.

Contributions.Title: Deep Dive Python: Techniques and Best Practices for Developers
Contributions.Description:
Deep Dive Python explains advanced Python concepts using real world open source code from popular Python repositories. 

Topics covered include core data structures, typing, generators, iterators, the itertools module, multithreading, multiprocessing, and asyncio - with examples of how they are applied in production-level code.

Contributions.Title: Understanding Model Context Protocol (MCP): Revolutionizing LLM Integration | Datahour AV
Contributions.Description:
Curious about the buzz around 'Model Context Protocol' (MCP)? This groundbreaking technology is revolutionizing how language models interact with external knowledge, ensuring more transparent and reliable AI systems. In this session, we’ll break down how MCP works, its key benefits, and potential drawbacks. We’ll also dive into real-world examples to see MCP in action and walk you through building your very own simple MCP server. Whether you're a developer or an AI enthusiast, this session will equip you with practical insights to leverage MCP effectively.

Key Takeaways:

- Learn how the Model Context Protocol (MCP) enhances LLM interactions with external knowledge.
- Understand MCP's key benefits, such as improved reliability, transparency, and scalability.
- Explore real-world applications of MCP in building robust AI systems.
- Gain hands-on experience by building a simple MCP server during the session.

Contributions.Title: Optimizando el coste de nuestro Cosmos

Sin embargo, ¿realmente es tan caro para implementarlo en todos los proyectos, o quizás no lo es tanto? En este artículo, intentaremos ofrecer una serie de consejos para comprender mejor todo lo que Azure nos ofrece y así poder pagar lo necesario en nuestro desarrollo.

Contributions.Title: Uso de Contenedores para un Developer de Office 365
Contributions.Description: Hoy en día el uso de los contenedores está ligado a aplicaciones que necesiten un nivel de escalado y aprovechamiento al máximo de la infraestructura en la que se ejecutan. No hay una buena arquitectura que no diga Microservicios, Kubernetes, CQRS sino se quiere decir que está a la última. Tenemos ligado el uso de un contenedor a la plataforma donde se ejecuta nuestro desarrollo. Sin embargo, el uso de los contenedores no es solamente importante por ser la plataforma donde se ejecutan tienen muchos más usos que pueden ser más importantes en el proceso de desarrollo de una aplicación y que facilita la vida a un desarrollador. En este artículo abordaremos esta funcionalidad de los contenedores, porque debemos utilizarlos en nuestro día a día como desarrolladores de Office 365 y que problemática nos soluciona (también alguna complejidad nos da, pero como todo en esta vida está en el equilibrio).

Contributions.Title: Introducing PIPE — LLM Computing Primitive for Developers
Contributions.Description:
"Did we really just do; what we really just did?" was the feeling when I built the first version of a computing primitive in the LLM space. It's called a pipe. Prompt Instructions Personalization Engine. A pipe is a language and framework-agnostic; it's like a serverless function, and it works anywhere with virtually any LLM, like a small mixture of expert models. Simple, right? Not exactly rocket science, not groundbreaking, but oh-so-powerful.

It scales so well, I couldn't believe it myself. 984 million tokens generated in three weeks. It's an undeniably brilliant full-stack AI developer experience.

In this talk, I'll explain why an LLM Pipe is genuinely a winning bet and how it will change the way we build gen-AI use cases with LLMs. I'll also discuss how you can create your own custom AI operating system on top of Langbase.

By the end of this talk, you'll walk away prepared to build your own generative AI LLM web apps in less than five minutes. You'll feel confident building any

Contributions.Title: European SharePoint Conference 2024 :: Running Secure Power Automate Flows
Contributions.Description:
Have you ever wondered how to call APIs from Power Automate cloud flows securely? Or what are the different approaches to secure your API calls? Power Automate cloud flows can be turned into APIs, but how can you secure these flowy endpoints? Or have you ever wondered how you can mask the data running in your cloud flows? All of these questions and more will be answered in this session.

In this deep dive session, you will learn how you can secure your Power Automate cloud flows when using HTTP calls from within Power Automate and how to employ Azure key vaults. This session also provides prescriptive guidance on how you can secure your Power Automate flows that act like APIs!

Join Ahmad Najjar in an advanced-level session that will extend your Power Automate development skills beyond the point-and-click!

そのような方や、これからグループ ポリシーの管理を行なおうという方のために、グループ ポリシーの原理や運用の基本を解説しています。

Contributions.Title: .NET Conf 2025 .NETラボ 勉強会 2025年2月・Windows のセキュリティ強化 - 管理者保護（Administrator protection）モード
Contributions.Description:

このセッションでは管理者保護（Administrator protection）モードの動作と原理、構成方法について解説します。

mvps.Name: Amit Jain
mvps.Headline: Chartered Accountant & Excel Expert | Passionate Trainer & Content Creator
mvps.Biography: I am Amit Jain, a Chartered Accountant with total 17+ years of experience in finance, accounting, and reporting. I did not know much about Excel when I started my career. My passion for Excel emerged during my corporate journey when I realized the need to master this tool for efficient performance. Subsequently, I began automating processes and training teams in addition to my regular duties, which led to recognition and career growth. To fill this gap by sharing my knowledge, I started creating content on YouTube, conducting webinars, and developing courses. I founded a community for Excel learners, offering learning sessions and over time, added Q&A sessions & challenges to enhance skills. For the past 8+ years, I have been a full-time Excel expert and content creator, sharing Excel knowledge, developing automated tools to save professionals' time, training users on Excel, and working as a corporate consultant.

Contributions.Title: Top 5 Must-Know Excel Functions for Finance Professionals | Live with Real-World Examples
Contributions.Description: This live session was for Finance Professionals who want to speed up financial analysis and reporting. I covered the 5 Excel Functions that every finance expert must master. Whether people are working on financial modeling, forecasting, or data analysis – these functions will save time, reduce errors, and enhance efficiency. It was with real world eamples and with pro tim to maximize efficiency.

Contributions.Title: How to create automated financials in Excel using Tally
Contributions.Description: In this video tutorial, I demonstrated how to automate financial reporting in Excel using data exported from Tally, a popular accounting software. Viewers learned step-by-step instructions on importing Tally data into Excel and setting up automated formulas and functions to generate financial reports such as balance sheets, income statement. By using the power of Excel and Tally integration, users can streamline their financial analysis and reporting processes, saving time and improving accuracy.

Contributions.Title: How to create Dashboard in Excel
Contributions.Description: In this webinar, I talked about why data is important for making decisions. I also showed how to make a dashboard in Excel to help visualize data better for decision-making. I used various Excel functions like Pivot tables, charts, slicers, and shapes to demonstrate how to do this effectively.

Contributions.Title: Advanced Vlookup Excel for People in Corporate Part 3
Contributions.Description: In this video tutorial, I explained the dynamic usage of VLOOKUP with the MATCH function in Excel. Viewers learned how to combine these powerful functions to efficiently search for and retrieve data from a table based on specific criteria. By understanding the dynamic capabilities of VLOOKUP and MATCH, users can create flexible and robust formulas.

Contributions.Title: GSTR 2A in Excel in few easy steps
Contributions.Description: In this video, I provided assistance to individuals struggling to reconcile records under the new GST system in India. Using Excel, I demonstrated a straightforward method to reconcile GST data efficiently with just a few simple steps. Viewers learned practical techniques to streamline their reconciliation process, helping them navigate the challenges posed by the introduction of GST seamlessly.

Contributions.Title: ProvePresent: Ending Proxy Attendance with Azure Serverless & Azure OpenAI
Contributions.Description: ProvePresent is an Azure‑powered system that stops proxy attendance in schools. It uses rotating QR codes, SignalR live presence checks, Azure OpenAI‑generated quizzes from lecture slides, and AI‑based seating verification from student‑captured photos. It ensures students are physically in the room and paying attention—turning attendance from a weak checkbox into a verifiable, interactive learning experience.

Contributions.Title: Azure Adventure Unleashed: Enhancing Learning with an Azure OpenAI HTML5 RPG Game
Contributions.Description: The updates made to Azure Adventure have significantly enhanced the game's functionality, security, and overall user experience. The utilization of Azure Open AI ChatGPT 3.5 has allowed for the generation of dynamic and engaging instructions, making the game more enjoyable for students. The addition of the Azure Static Website Managed API and Active Directory Authentication has improved the game's functionality and provided an extra layer of security. The deployment process has also been streamlined and made more efficient using the Cloud Development Kit for Terraform (CDKTF). These updates have made Azure Adventure a more immersive and user-friendly game for students, while also providing educators with valuable insights into the duration of each task in the Azure assignments. Overall, the updates have been successful in improving the game and ensuring a better user experience for all players.

Contributions.Title: The Power of AI in Grading Handwriting Assignments
Contributions.Description: The traditional process of grading tests and assignments can be a tedious and time-consuming task for educators. However, with the application of machine learning and AI methods, this process can be automated and made more efficient. The scoring package presented in this article offers a solution that utilizes OCR, embedding, and similarity algorithms to automatically score student answers. The program also includes a web form that allows educators to input scores and track grading progress. With this package, educators can reduce the workload of grading and ensure that scoring is objective and fair. Additionally, the program generates a report and individual scored script pdf files that can be emailed to students. This scoring package is a significant improvement over the traditional grading process in 8x times speed and offers a more efficient and objective way of scoring student assignments.

Contributions.Title: Empowering Educators: Automated Assignment Scoring via Azure OpenAI Service ChatGPT
Contributions.Description: Educators should consider utilizing AI technology, such as Azure OpenAI Service ChatGPT, to assist with or automate grading tasks. This AI demonstrates consistent and reliable reasoning and processing capabilities, delivering impressive results in automatic assignment evaluation. Furthermore, it can analyze student responses, providing valuable insights to help enhance their learning experience.

Contributions.Title: Automatic Grading with Azure OpenAI Services ChatGPT Virtual Assistant
Contributions.Description:
Introduction
Why waste time grading assignments manually when AI can do it in seconds?
It’s impossible to stop students from using AI tools to answer their assignments, and it’s nonsensical to ask them to do something that AI can complete in a second. The key is to ensure that students are working on their assignments on their own and not copying from others, then using AI to rephrase the language. In fact, it’s nonsensical for teachers to score student work manually when all students use AI to prepare their assignments! The ideal solution is to let AI grade it automatically.

At IVE we have created an AI virtual assistant that can help students with their assignments. The assistant can record chats and/or screen activities during tests. However, we are aware of the environmental and economic challenges of using AI, as it consumes a lot of energy and there is a cost of using the AI Service which students should be aware of as part of Responsible AI.

Contributions.Title: Microsoft Reactor: Analyzing Microsoft Fabric Data using an Azure AI Foundry Agent
Contributions.Description: In this session, you'll learn how to deploy an AI agent that interacts with Microsoft Fabric datasets, automating insights generation and anomaly detection. Discover how this agent can serve as a conversational interface for business users, bridging the gap between raw data and strategic decision-making.

Contributions.Title: Microsoft Reactor: Analyzing Customer Call Recordings with Content Understanding & Microsoft Fabric
Contributions.Description:
Dive into the world of voice data analytics with Azure AI Content Understanding and Microsoft Fabric.

We will leverage the generative AI capabilities of Azure AI Content Understanding to intelligently extract insights, entities, and patterns from customer call recordings—tailored to the specific information we want to uncover. This session also demonstrates how to build an automated pipeline in Microsoft Fabric that orchestrates the ingestion, transformation, and analysis of voice data, enabling scalable, real-time insights that improve service quality and enhance customer experience strategies.

Contributions.Title: Microsoft Reactor: Transforming and Analyzing Customer Reviews with Microsoft Foundry and Fabric
Contributions.Description: This session explores how unstructured review data can be transformed into actionable intelligence through Azure AI Language services, including sentiment analysis and opinion mining. Learn how to build scalable pipelines using Azure AI Foundry and Microsoft Fabric to orchestrate these capabilities and integrate them with Fabric’s Lakehouse architecture to drive customer-centric decisions with precision and agility.
